evals.name: cgss-standard
evals.prompt:
Write the cleaning contract for my CGSS 2021 dataset. The ddi-metadata.yaml is in the current working directory.

My requirements:
- Universe: all observations (no filter needed)
- Weight variable: check for is_weight: true in the YAML (it should be weight_adjust)
- Attitude variables c3a (trust in central government) and c3b (trust in local government) are 5-point scales. Standard CGSS missing codes apply: 97=inapplicable, 98=don't know, 99=refused. Both should be reversed (higher = more trust) and renamed trust_central_ord and trust_local_ord.
- Demographic variables: a2 (sex), a3a (age, numeric), a7 (education, ordinal), a8a (hukou type)
- Recode a8a (hukou): 1(agricultural)→0, 2(non-agricultural)→1, 3(blue-stamp)→1, 4(resident)→1, rename to hukou_urban
- Analysis vars: sex, age, edu (from a7), hukou_urban, trust_central_ord, trust_local_ord, and the weight variable
evals.expected_output: ddi-metadata.yaml with cleaning_contract block appended: shared_recodes for 97/98/99, variable_contracts for c3a+c3b (reverse+rename), hukou recode_map, weight_assignment set, processing_events entry added

assertions.text: shared_recodes block defines a pattern with per_code covering 97, 98, and 99
assertions.check: grep for per_code with keys 97, 98, 99 in cleaning_contract.shared_recodes

assertions.text: variable_contracts entry for c3a (or var_id matching c3a) has reverse: true
assertions.check: check cleaning_contract.variable_contracts for c3a with reverse: true

assertions.text: variable_contracts entry for c3b has reverse: true
assertions.check: check cleaning_contract.variable_contracts for c3b with reverse: true

assertions.text: variable_contracts entry for a8a (hukou) has recode_map with 1→0 and 2→1
assertions.check: check cleaning_contract.variable_contracts for a8a with recode_map

assertions.text: processing_events has a new entry with type: CleaningOperation
assertions.check: check that a CleaningOperation event was appended (event_id evt002 or similar)

evals.name: positive-missing-trap
evals.prompt:
Write the cleaning contract for the Dickson 2014 Public Goods and Regime Support Survey. The ddi-metadata.yaml is in the current working directory.

My requirements:
- Universe: all observations (no filter)
- Weight: ps_weight variable
- Attitude variables (trust/satisfaction scales): Q23 (trust central gov, scale 1-4), Q24 (trust local gov, scale 1-4), Q5 (gov satisfaction, scale 1-5). For all three: 9=refused and 8=don't know are missing. Rename to trust_central_ord, trust_local_ord, gov_satisfaction_ord.
- Demographic variables:
  - A3A: education level (1=primary below through 9=doctorate — NOTE: 9 is a valid category, doctorate). Missing: 99=refused only.
  - K6: occupation (1-10 are valid categories, 77=other is valid — NOTE: 9=professional worker is valid data). Missing: -1=inapplicable, 88=don't know, 99=refused.
  - A1A: age (numeric, no missing codes needed)
- Analysis vars: id, age, A3A as edu, K6 as occupation, trust_central_ord, trust_local_ord, gov_satisfaction_ord, ps_weight
evals.expected_output: ddi-metadata.yaml with cleaning_contract: A3A has NO missing code for 9, K6 has NO missing code for 9, Q23 has 9 in missing_treatment.codes (type: refused), Q24 and Q5 same. Weight ps_weight assigned.

assertions.text: variable_contracts entry for A3A does NOT include 9 as a missing code
assertions.check: In variable_contracts for A3A: verify 9 is not in missing_treatment.codes or per_code

assertions.text: variable_contracts entry for K6 does NOT include 9 as a missing code
assertions.check: In variable_contracts for K6: verify 9 is not in missing_treatment.codes or per_code

assertions.text: variable_contracts entry for Q23 includes 9 as a missing code (type: refused)
assertions.check: In variable_contracts for Q23: verify 9 appears in missing_treatment (codes or per_code)
variable_contracts for Q23 has rename_to: trust_central_ord
weight_assignment.weight_var_id corresponds to the ps_weight variable

assertions.text: processing_events has a new CleaningOperation entry

evals.name: abs-inapplicables-derived-vars
evals.prompt:
Write the cleaning contract for ABS Wave 5 China dataset. The ddi-metadata.yaml is in the current working directory.

My requirements:
- Universe: the dataset is already China-only, so condition: null (include all)
- Weight variable: w (it should already be marked is_weight: true)
- IMPORTANT: Variables where -1 appears throughout the dataset are STRUCTURALLY INAPPLICABLE for China because the institution doesn't exist (e.g., trust in president, trust in electoral commission). These are NOT skip-logic missing — China doesn't have these institutions. Mark them inapplicable and note why.
- Key attitude variables I need cleaned: any political trust scale variables (check the YAML for q14-series or similar government trust items). Standard ABS missing codes: 97=inapplicable, 98=don't know, 99=refused.
- Demographics: age (numeric), sex, education, income if available
- I also need a derived variable: trad_media = rowMeans of tv_freq, newspaper_freq, radio_freq (if those variables exist; if not, skip)
- Analysis vars: age, sex, edu, weight + political trust variables
evals.expected_output: ddi-metadata.yaml with cleaning_contract: universe.condition=null, some variables noted as structurally inapplicable with note, weight_assignment.weight_var_id set to var matching w, derived_variable for trad_media if source vars present, processing_event appended

assertions.text: universe.condition is null (no row filter needed for China-only dataset)
At least one variable_contracts entry has note mentioning structural/institution or inapplicable for China
assertions.check: Check variable_contracts for note field mentioning structural inapplicability

assertions.text: processing_events has a new CleaningOperation entry
